(born 14 May 1974) is an English lawn and indoor bowler.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) He reached a career high ranking of world number 19 in August 2024.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

**Nick Brett** (born 14 May 1974) is an English lawn and indoor bowler.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) He reached a career high ranking of world number 19 in August 2024.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Bowls career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nick_Brett&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Bowls career")]

Brett bowls for the Huntingdon indoor bowls club and Brampton outdoor bowls club. He was ranked the indoor world number 1 in 2015 and 2016.[[5]](#cite_note-WBT-5) and also won an [outdoors National Title](/wiki/List_of_Bowls_England_Men%27s_National_Championships "List of Bowls England Men's National Championships") in 1999 and subsequent singles at the [British Isles Bowls Championships](/wiki/British_Isles_Bowls_Championships "British Isles Bowls Championships") in 2000.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

Brett's greatest achievement before 2016 was winning the [2014 World Indoor Bowls Championship](/wiki/2014_World_Indoor_Bowls_Championship "2014 World Indoor Bowls Championship") Pairs title partnering [Greg Harlow](/wiki/Greg_Harlow "Greg Harlow").[[7]](#cite_note-7) It was during the [2016 World Indoor Bowls Championship](/wiki/2016_World_Indoor_Bowls_Championship "2016 World Indoor Bowls Championship") that Brett became the World Singles Champion for the first time beating [Robert Paxton](/wiki/Robert_Paxton_(bowls) "Robert Paxton (bowls)") in a competitive final.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

Brett followed up his 2016 success by winning the mixed pairs title at the [2017 World Indoor Bowls Championship](/wiki/2017_World_Indoor_Bowls_Championship "2017 World Indoor Bowls Championship") with [Claire Johnston](/wiki/Claire_Johnston_(bowls) "Claire Johnston (bowls)") of Scotland.[[9]](#cite_note-9) He then joined an exclusive group of players who have won five or more world indoor titles after winning both the open pairs and mixed pairs at the [2020 World Indoor Bowls Championship](/wiki/2020_World_Indoor_Bowls_Championship "2020 World Indoor Bowls Championship")[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11) and he narrowly failed to become the first player to win the treble after losing to Paxton in the open singles final. Other major indoor wins include the 2015 [International Open](/wiki/World_Bowls_Tour_Events "World Bowls Tour Events") and the 2013[[12]](#cite_note-12) and 2014 [Scottish International Open](/wiki/World_Bowls_Tour_Events "World Bowls Tour Events").[[13]](#cite_note-13)

In 2020, he was selected for the [2020 World Outdoor Bowls Championship](/wiki/2020_World_Outdoor_Bowls_Championship "2020 World Outdoor Bowls Championship") in Australia but the event was cancelled due to the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic").[[14]](#cite_note-14) World ranked number one indoors he was unable to participate in the [2021 World Indoor Bowls Championship](/wiki/2021_World_Indoor_Bowls_Championship "2021 World Indoor Bowls Championship") because he broke his arm following an accident playing golf.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

In 2022, he competed in the [men's triples](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_at_the_2022_Commonwealth_Games_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_triples "Lawn bowls at the 2022 Commonwealth Games – Men's triples") and the [men's fours](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_at_the_2022_Commonwealth_Games_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_fours "Lawn bowls at the 2022 Commonwealth Games – Men's fours") at the [2022 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/2022_Commonwealth_Games "2022 Commonwealth Games").[[16]](#cite_note-16) The team of Brett, [Louis Ridout](/wiki/Louis_Ridout "Louis Ridout") and [Jamie Chestney](/wiki/Jamie_Chestney "Jamie Chestney") won the triples gold medal and in the fours he also secured a bronze medal.[[17]](#cite_note-17) The success during 2022 continued when he secured a second national title, winning the 2022 pairs with Lewis Baker.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

At the [2023 World Indoor Bowls Championship](/wiki/2023_World_Indoor_Bowls_Championship "2023 World Indoor Bowls Championship"), Brett won the Open Pairs with Greg Harlow again, defeating [Jason Greenslade](/wiki/Jason_Greenslade "Jason Greenslade") and [Michael Stepney](/wiki/Michael_Stepney "Michael Stepney") in the final. It was Brett's sixth title over all disciplines.[[19]](#cite_note-19)

In 2023, he was selected as part of the team to represent England at the [2023 World Outdoor Bowls Championship](/wiki/2023_World_Outdoor_Bowls_Championship "2023 World Outdoor Bowls Championship").[[20]](#cite_note-20) He participated in the [men's triples](/wiki/2023_World_Outdoor_Bowls_Championship_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_Triples "2023 World Outdoor Bowls Championship – Men's Triples") and the [men's fours](/wiki/2023_World_Outdoor_Bowls_Championship_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_Fours "2023 World Outdoor Bowls Championship – Men's Fours") events.[[21]](#cite_note-21)[[22]](#cite_note-22) In the triples with [Jamie Walker](/wiki/Jamie_Walker_(bowls) "Jamie Walker (bowls)") and [Louis Ridout](/wiki/Louis_Ridout "Louis Ridout"), he won a bronze medal.

Brett started 2024 in good form winning the [2024 World Indoor Bowls Championship](/wiki/2024_World_Indoor_Bowls_Championship "2024 World Indoor Bowls Championship") mixed pairs with [Julie Forrest](/wiki/Julie_Forrest "Julie Forrest"), as the pair bowled together for the first time in the Championships.[[23]](#cite_note-23)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nick_Brett&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Brett is a supervisor by trade and is married with two children.[[5]](#cite_note-WBT-5) Due to injury in 2021 he was unable to compete in the World Indoor Championships and consequently joined the [commentary team](/wiki/Sports_commentator "Sports commentator") for the event.
